creator: fernan.aguero@gmail.com
text:
<precise description of the problem (multiple lines)>

        Hi, don't know what the problem is ... I can fetch the port from
        the listed MASTER_SITES and the checksum of the downloaded file
        matches the distinfo.

        Anyone care to check manually? (I'm CCing miwi, as s/he was
        responsible of the last commit).

        The logs at pointyhat (August 2009, before the port was marked
        BROKEN) show that the build was fetching the file from the old
        MASTER_SITE (ftp://ftp.cgb.ki.se/pub/esr/dotter/). I sent a PR
        with a new MASTER site around the same time (see
        http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=137840), and after
        that point there are no more errorlogs from pointyhat.

        http://pointyhat.freebsd.org/errorlogs/i386-errorlogs/e.6.20090822221417/dotter-20021204.log
        http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/cvsweb.cgi/ports/biology/dotter/Makefile?rev=1.10;content-type=text/plain

        On the other hand, if you take a look at the history of the port
        (http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/cvsweb.cgi/ports/biology/dotter/Makefile)
        you would notice that lately, without any change to the port's
        files, the port has been marked broken, and was later marked
        clean, due to fetch/checksum issues. Probably because the
        MASTER_SITE went offline for a while?

        Maybe a solution would be to put a copy of the distfile in
        FreeBSD's FTP repository?

Fix: <how to correct or work around the problem, if known (multiple lines)>





-- 
fernan--bvOYsFAN2CWvNaslKTdoyEBPyM94ybskmhgyzRQxWgBsu6NH
Content-Type: text/plain; name="dotter.diff"
Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit
Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="dotter.diff"

diff --exclude CVS -ruN /usr/ports/biology/dotter/Makefile dotter/Makefile
--- /usr/ports/biology/dotter/Makefile  2010-03-14 08:07:11.000000000 -0300
+++ dotter/Makefile     2010-03-14 08:09:29.000000000 -0300
@@ -21,8 +21,6 @@
 USE_LINUX=     yes
 ONLY_FOR_ARCHS=        i386

-BROKEN=                checksum mismatch
-
 NO_BUILD=      yes
 PLIST_FILES=   bin/${PORTNAME} bin/${DISTNAME}
How-To-Repeat:         <code/input/activities to reproduce the problem (multiple lines)>
